brand.name: USOO
brand.vertical: SaaS/tech — B2C consumer dating app (video speed dating, 18+)

summary: USOO is a video speed-dating app where singles join live, structured 5-minute face-to-face video calls with compatible matches, then independently Like or Pass — mutual likes unlock messaging, icebreaker games, and social chat rooms. It serves swipe-fatigued daters aged ~22–38 who want to feel real chemistry on camera before investing time in someone. The model is freemium (1 date/day free) converting to monthly Pro ($30) and Premium ($50) subscriptions.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation — get the new signup past video anxiety and into their first live 5-minute speed date, racing toward the 'It's a Match!' aha moment.
campaigns.trigger: Account created / signup completed (age gate passed). Send instantly — welcome opens drop ~50% if delayed.
campaigns.segment: All new signups; branch by whether a profile photo/bio has been added vs. account is bare.
campaigns.rationale: USOO is a novel format in beta — the single biggest risk is a signup who never joins a session because being on camera with a stranger feels scary. The welcome email has to convert curiosity into one brave click before the nerves win.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, normalize the camera nerves in one line, and drive the single most important first action: join one live 5-minute date today.
emails.subject: You're in. Your first 5 minutes could change everything.
emails.preview: No swiping, no 'hey' that goes nowhere — just one real conversation.
keyPoints: Reassure: it's only 5 minutes, both people opted in, and you can Pass with zero awkwardness — frame the nerves as the normal price of something real
Set the expectation that chemistry is felt fast on video, and the aha is the mutual match — most people get there within their first 1–3 sessions
One button, one action: join a live date now (free tier, 1/day); reply-to is a real human if they're nervous
Soft nudge to finish profile photo + bio first if missing, so they show up as their real self

campaigns.name: Freemium-to-Pro Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert engaged free users into paying Pro/Premium subscribers at the moment the 1-date/day cap frustrates them.
campaigns.trigger: Free user hits the daily 1-date cap (primary milestone); parallel expiry-style nudge for users who matched but can't message on free tier.
campaigns.segment: Active free users; branch by depth — capped-and-engaged (push more dates) vs. matched-but-blocked (push messaging unlock).
campaigns.rationale: The freemium cap is USOO's entire revenue engine — the paywall fires exactly when desire peaks. A capped user who just felt a spark and wants 'one more date' or wants to message a match is the warmest possible upgrade moment, and right now nothing catches them.

emails.purpose: Catch the user the moment they hit the daily cap, validate the momentum they're feeling, and make upgrading to Pro the obvious way to keep going today.
emails.subject: That was your last free date today. Want 24 more?
emails.preview: You're clearly into this. Pro unlocks 25 dates a day + messaging your matches.
keyPoints: Acknowledge the win: you came back, you're feeling it — don't let the clock kill the momentum
Pro = 25 speed dates/day + message your matches + icebreaker games for $30/mo, cancel anytime; tease Premium (unlimited, social rooms) for the all-in
Frame against swipe apps: the cost of one bad first date vs. a month of real, fast connections
Reference cancel-anytime and the refund policy to lower commitment fear; no guarantees of romantic outcomes

campaigns.name: Session Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize the share of registered users who actually show up to their scheduled speed-date session, and re-book the ones who don't.
campaigns.trigger: User registers/reserves a session slot; upcoming-session window; no-show event after a missed slot.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled or reserved speed-date session; separate no-penalty branch for no-shows.
campaigns.rationale: Showing up IS the revenue event for a video app — a registered user who ghosts their own session never reaches the match aha and is the likeliest to churn. Reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average, and a warm nudge directly counters the camera anxiety that makes people bail at the last second.

emails.purpose: Pre-session reminder that lowers video anxiety and gets the user to actually click into their live date at the scheduled time.
emails.subject: Your speed date starts soon — deep breath, you've got this
emails.preview: 5 minutes, good lighting, just be you. Singles are queuing up now.
keyPoints: Confirm the exact time and a one-tap join link; remind it's only 5 minutes and they control Like/Pass
Quick anti-anxiety prep: face a window or light, decent wifi, smile — and a reminder that the other person is just as nervous
Reinforce stakes are low and upside is high: this could be the 2 minutes you finally feel a spark
If now's bad, one-click reschedule so a wobble becomes a re-book instead of a no-show

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant free users and lapsed subscribers, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (e.g., no session joined in 30–60 days) or subscription cancellation.
campaigns.segment: Dormant free users vs. churned paid subscribers (different message); lean on real, waiting-match signals where available.
campaigns.rationale: Dating is uniquely win-back-friendly — a single concrete signal ('someone Liked you', 'new singles in your area') is a far stronger pull than any generic 'we miss you'. It's fast to ship and recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts while protecting the young beta list's sender reputation.

emails.purpose: Reignite a dormant user with a specific, true reason to come back — a waiting match or fresh activity — rather than guilt.
emails.subject: Someone's been hoping you'd come back
emails.preview: New singles are joining USOO every day — your next 5 minutes is waiting.
keyPoints: Lead with a concrete pull: unread match / new compatible singles since you left (use real data; never fabricate a person)
Remind them why they joined — they were done with swiping and wanted something real; that's still here
Make returning frictionless: one tap back into a live date, free tier still active
Tone is warm and no-pressure, not needy; sets up a later sunset email for true non-responders to protect deliverability

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter — The USOO Spark
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind, build the brand-love that makes USOO a habit, and keep free users warm for conversion — without discounting.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route recently-dormant users to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: A beta dating brand lives or dies on belief that the format actually works — recurring success stories and confidence-building dating tips are the cheapest, most authentic proof, and they quietly keep low-engagement free users on the list until their conversion moment arrives.

emails.purpose: Flagship edition built around a real USOO match story (social proof) plus one practical tip, proving the 5-minute format delivers and nudging readers back into a session.
emails.subject: They matched in 5 minutes. Here's exactly how.
emails.preview: A real USOO spark story + one tip to make your next date land.
emails.cta: Try it on your next date
keyPoints: Lead story: a real (consented, anonymized) member match — what those first 2 minutes on camera actually felt like
One actionable tip: how to open a 5-minute date so the conversation clicks (e.g., skip 'hey', ask one real question)
Recurring 'This week's spark' section to build a reading habit; 80% value, 20% soft nudge to join a date
Single primary CTA back into the app; no outcome guarantees, inclusive and warm voice throughout
